stemHtml: A pilot suffers from common shortage of sleep, as they often wake up after only a few hours of rest, and cannot return to sleep. Which of the following should be taken into account before beginning the use of sleep medication?

options.a: Drugs to improve sleep may be used during flight, if approved by the commander.
options.b: Sleep-inducing drugs may be taken up to 4 hours before flight, without needing to consult an Aeromedical Examiner.
options.c: Sleep-improving drugs can be taken during flight duty, but only just before a short in-flight rest period.
options.d: The use of drugs containing melatonin within 12 hours of, or during, flight duty, is prohibited.
